comment: Math primitives for the special-function cluster. Definitions, recurrences, and orthogonality weights from NIST DLMF (https://dlmf.nist.gov). Section URLs in `notes` field per cluster A5 citation grain. Render uses inline TS implementations (Lanczos Γ, series+asymptotic Bessel, Bonnet/recursion-based orthogonal polynomials).

primitives.name: Gamma function
primitives.notes: DLMF §5.2 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/5.2). Renderer uses Lanczos g=7 approximation; reflection formula handles Re(z) < 1/2.

primitives.name: Beta function
primitives.notes: DLMF §5.12 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/5.12). Plotted as Β(x, 2) on x ∈ (0, 4].

primitives.name: Bessel function of the first kind

attrs.definingEquation: x² y'' + x y' + (x² - n²) y = 0  →  y = J_n(x)

primitives.notes: DLMF §10.2-10.6 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/10.2). Plot shows J_0, J_1, J_2 on x ∈ [0, 20]. Implementation: power series for |x| < 12, Hankel asymptotic above.

primitives.name: Bessel function of the second kind

attrs.definingEquation: x² y'' + x y' + (x² - n²) y = 0  →  y = Y_n(x)

primitives.notes: DLMF §10.2 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/10.2). Logarithmic singularity at x → 0+. Plot shows Y_0, Y_1 on x ∈ [0.5, 20].

primitives.name: Modified Bessel function of the first kind

attrs.definingEquation: x² y'' + x y' - (x² + n²) y = 0  →  y = I_n(x)

primitives.notes: DLMF §10.25 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/10.25). Plot shows I_0, I_1 on x ∈ [0, 4].

primitives.name: Modified Bessel function of the second kind

attrs.definingEquation: x² y'' + x y' - (x² + n²) y = 0  →  y = K_n(x)

primitives.notes: DLMF §10.25 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/10.25). Asymptotic K_n(x) ~ √(π/2x) e^(-x) used for x > 5; series elsewhere.

primitives.name: Legendre polynomial P_n(x)
primitives.notes: DLMF §14.7 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/14.7). Plot shows P_2, P_3, P_4 on x ∈ [-1, 1].

primitives.name: Legendre function of the second kind Q_n(x)

attrs.definingEquation: (1-x²) y'' - 2x y' + n(n+1) y = 0,  second linearly-independent solution

primitives.notes: DLMF §14.7 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/14.7). Q_0(x) = ½ ln((1+x)/(1-x)). Plot shows Q_0, Q_1, Q_2 on x ∈ (-0.95, 0.95).

primitives.name: Associated Legendre function P_l^m(x)

attrs.orthogonalityWeight: 1 on [-1, 1] (fixed m); generates spherical harmonics via the φ phase factor

primitives.notes: DLMF §14.3, §14.10 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/14.3). Plot shows P_2^1 and P_3^2 on x ∈ [-1, 1].

primitives.name: Hermite polynomial (physicists') H_n(x)

attrs.orthogonalityWeight: e^(-x²) on (-∞, ∞);  ∫ H_m H_n e^(-x²) dx = √π 2^n n! δ_{mn}

primitives.notes: DLMF §18.5, §18.7 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/18.5). Physicists' (not probabilists') normalization. Plot shows H_2, H_3, H_4 on x ∈ [-3, 3].

primitives.name: Generalised Laguerre polynomial L_n^α(x)

attrs.orthogonalityWeight: x^α e^(-x) on (0, ∞);  ∫₀^∞ L_m^α L_n^α x^α e^(-x) dx = Γ(n+α+1)/n! δ_{mn}

primitives.notes: DLMF §18.3, §18.9 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/18.3). Hydrogen radial wavefunction uses L_{n-l-1}^{2l+1}. Plot shows L_1, L_2, L_3 (α=0) on x ∈ [0, 10].

primitives.name: Gegenbauer polynomial C_n^α(x)
primitives.notes: DLMF §18.7 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/18.7). Generalises Legendre (α=1/2) and Chebyshev (α=0,1). Plot shows C_2, C_3, C_4 (α=1/2) on x ∈ [-1, 1].

attrs.orthogonalityWeight: sin θ dθ dφ on the unit sphere; ∫ Y_l^m* Y_{l'}^{m'} dΩ = δ_{ll'} δ_{mm'}

primitives.name: Gauss hypergeometric ₂F₁(a, b; c; z)
primitives.notes: DLMF §15 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/15). Plot shows ₂F₁(1/2, 1/2; 1; z) on z ∈ (-0.95, 0.95) — the complete elliptic integral kernel.

primitives.name: Confluent hypergeometric ₁F₁(a; b; z)

attrs.recurrence: z y'' + (b - z) y' - a y = 0  (Kummer's equation)

primitives.notes: DLMF §13 (https://dlmf.nist.gov/13). Plot shows ₁F₁(1; 2; z) = (e^z - 1)/z on z ∈ [-3, 3].
